component.name: Bulwark
component.summary: Security and antivirus scanner for Linux

description.p: Bulwark scans a Linux host for security misconfigurations and intrusion indicators using a native Rust rule engine over declarative YAML rules, and explains findings in plain language with a suggested fix.

ul.li: System configuration — SSH, sudo, kernel parameters, cron, file permissions
Persistence and intrusion indicators, including promiscuous network interfaces
File integrity against a recorded baseline
AI coding assistants, for API keys leaked into transcripts and for agent configuration a prompt injection could turn into code execution
Viruses and malware, using ClamAV when it is installed

description.p: Findings are sorted by how much they matter, and many come with a reversible fix that previews its changes before applying them — tightened file permissions, hardened SSH settings, redacted secrets — each shown as a preview first, so you decide before anything changes.

Bulwark keeps watching after the first scan. It re-runs its checks on a timer, notices when a watched file changes, and tells you only when something genuinely new appears rather than repeating what you have already seen. A history view shows how the machine's posture has moved over time.
Everything runs locally. The scanning engine makes no network calls and sends no telemetry; findings stay in a database on your own machine.

Notes for this Flatpak build: Bulwark reads the host read-only to audit it, and never writes to host configuration on its own. Virus scanning uses the ClamAV already installed on your system, so signatures stay whatever your distribution keeps current. Checks that need administrator access run on the host and require the Bulwark command-line tool to be installed there as well; without it, every check that does not need root still works.

screenshot.caption: An overall safety score, with findings sorted by how much they matter
Scanning AI coding assistants for leaked secrets and risky configuration
Hardening checks grouped by category, with a compliance score
ClamAV virus scanning with live per-file progress
Every check Bulwark runs, explained in plain language
Appearance, and how often Bulwark re-checks the machine on its own

description.p: Virus scanning and administrator checks now work in the sandboxed builds.

ul.li: Virus scanning uses the ClamAV already installed on your system, with its current signatures
Checks that need administrator access can now run
The interface no longer stutters — a rendering workaround that was slowing it down has been removed
Clearer explanations when a feature genuinely isn't available, instead of advice that cannot help

description.p: Fixes the application window being empty in the Flatpak and Snap builds.

ul.li: The interface now loads correctly instead of showing a sandbox error on an empty window
Restores the system tray icon, so monitoring keeps running when the window is closed
Opening or showing a flagged file works again

description.p: Fixes opening files from a finding, and restores the system tray icon.

ul.li: Opening or showing a flagged file works again instead of reporting a sandbox error
The system tray icon is back, so monitoring keeps running when the window is closed
Clearer message when a scan needs administrator access, which the sandbox cannot request

description.p: Fixes the application failing to start in this Flatpak build.

ul.li: The application no longer closes immediately on launch when the system tray library is unavailable; it now starts normally and runs without a tray icon
Security checks are found correctly again, so continuous monitoring works instead of being silently switched off
When no tray icon is available, closing the window now quits the application rather than hiding it out of reach

description.p: Fixes scan history not being saved in this Flatpak build.

ul.li: Findings are now stored in the application's own data directory, so scans are recorded correctly
Removes a sandbox permission that is no longer needed
Fixes the Ubuntu PPA build, so the command-line tool can be installed with apt

description.p: Packaging fixes for this Flatpak build.

ul.li: Adds screenshots and release notes to the store listing
Ships a higher-resolution application icon
Drops a sandbox permission the application does not use
Published to the Ubuntu PPA, with classic Snap and Flatpak packaging
Install-verification in CI asserts each package actually ships its rule pack
Browser-driven interface tests for the desktop app
Reversible fixes for file permissions and SSH server configuration, previewed before they are applied
Scan a remote machine over SSH, with an isolated per-host history
Secret-detection accuracy fixes, removing false positives from placeholders and test fixtures
